description: §57 wire absence envelope, NOT path. loadUser() returns User | not; the absent response is the §57.2 canonical envelope {"__scrml_absent": true} (NORMATIVE + impl#1-matched, so declared directly — impl-neutral). The client dual-decoder (§57.4) lowers it to scrml `not` (JS null per §42.5/§42.8); @user is null, the derived userLabel falls to "anonymous", so #who reads "Current: anonymous". Twin of optional-present. The empty/fallback render.
rationale: §57.2 scrml-absence is wire-encoded as {"__scrml_absent": true}; §57.4 the dual-decoder lowers it to scrml `not` (never the string "undefined"). §42.2.5 `is not` -> userLabel = the fallback. Absence snapshots as null, never undefined (§42.8).
